messages.text: Great! I found a relevant result. Let me fetch the NASA announcement page for the specific details.

messages.name: fetch_url
messages.content:
# NASA Announcement: Voyager 1 Enters Interstellar Space

On September 12, 2013, NASA held a press conference confirming that Voyager 1 had crossed the heliopause. The agency stated that the spacecraft actually made the crossing on or around August 25, 2012, based on retrospective analysis of particle flux and plasma density data.

The evidence for the crossing included a sharp drop in the count of solar wind particles reaching the spacecraft and a corresponding rise in galactic cosmic rays. Plasma wave instruments onboard detected the density of surrounding plasma jump by a factor of roughly 40, consistent with the denser interstellar medium.

Dr. Ed Stone, Voyager project scientist at Caltech, called it "a historic step" in human exploration.

messages.text: Perfect! I have the information I need.

args.answer: Voyager 1 crossed the heliopause on or around August 25, 2012. NASA confirmed this crossing in a press conference on September 12, 2013, based on retrospective analysis of particle flux and plasma density data that showed a sharp drop in solar wind particles and a corresponding rise in galactic cosmic rays.

run_result.final_answer: Voyager 1 crossed the heliopause on or around August 25, 2012. NASA confirmed this crossing in a press conference on September 12, 2013, based on retrospective analysis of particle flux and plasma density data that showed a sharp drop in solar wind particles and a corresponding rise in galactic cosmic rays.
